markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify UserManual entries update for every new feature/tool/primitive/API touched by WP-009. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualFreshnessFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualFreshnessFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: UserManual freshness now detects same-count page child-row tampering and reseed heals the drift using real PostgreSQL evidence; targeted compile, formatting, API regression, KB ready checklist, and adversarial review passed.

evidence.summary: Added a shared UserManual seed-child-row comparison used by both `check_freshness` and `upsert_page`, so a matching page content_hash no longer masks same-count section/anchor drift and reseed rewrites mismatched child rows.

RED proof first failed because same-count `user_manual_sections` tampering still reported `fresh: true`; GREEN proof passed after the fix. Adjacent API freshness route regression and targeted cargo check also passed. All proof used real PostgreSQL through `knowledge_pg_support`; no SQLite, Docker-default, external daemon, or mock-only proof was used.
evidence_refs: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils --test user_manual_freshness_fixture_tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture (RED before fix: failed as expected)

cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils --test user_manual_freshness_fixture_tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture (GREEN after fix: 1 passed)

cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils --test user_manual_api_tests -- mt204_freshness_current_then_stale_fixture --test-threads=1 --nocapture (1 passed)

git diff --check -- src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml src/backend/handshake_core/src/user_manual/store.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/user_manual/freshness.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/user_manual_freshness_fixture_tests.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/user_manual_storage_tests.rs (exit 0; CRLF notices only)

evidence.summary: KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.

Read-only adversarial review found no blocking MT-239 findings. It confirmed freshness and reseed both use the same child-row comparison, the new fixture uses real PostgreSQL and proves the false-PASS state, and residual anchor/position/LIST_CAP concerns are non-blocking validation risks.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
